title: host dns resolution broken with rootless podman
body:
### Issue Description

`aardvark-dns` cannot communicate with host dns server (systemd-resolved). seems `slirp4netns` built-in dns resolution is broken with `--enable-sandbox`, see [this issue](https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns/issues/315).

Since podman sets `10.0.2.3` as first `nameserver` in /etc/resolv.conf for `aardvark-dns`, `aardvark-dns` cannot communicate to host dns server (systemd-resolved), `aardvark-dns` communicate to next `nameserver` only after it gets `None()` (empty response) from 10.0.2.3, by the time it gets response from the second nameserver, the dns query requester (`ping`) in the container already timed out and failed.

The current workaround is to disable passing `--enable-sandbox` to `slirp4netns`. I dont know the relationship between `no_pivot_root` and `--enable-sandbox`, but setting `no_pivot_root = true` disables passing `--enable-sandbox` to `slirp4netns`.

```console
mkdir -p ~/.config/containers/containers.conf.d
echo -e "[engine]\nno_pivot_root = true\n" >~/.config/containers/containers.conf.d/disable-sandbox.conf
```
Until `slirp4netns` fix, `podman` should not pass `--enable-sandbox` by default to `slirp4netns` for rootless containers.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```console
podman network create testnet
podman container run --rm --rmi --network testnet docker.io/library/alpine ping -c 2 google.com
```

### Describe the results you received

given steps will fail to resolve `google.com` with `bad address`

### Describe the results you expected

ping command inside alpine should resolve `google.com` to an IP address

comments.body:
From the slirp4netns [man page](https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns/blob/master/slirp4netns.1.md):
> --enable-sandbox (since v0.4.0) enter the user namespace and create a new mount namespace where only /etc and /run are mounted from the host.
> Requires /etc/resolv.conf not to be a symlink to a file outside /etc and /run.
> When running as the root, the process does not enter the user namespace but all the capabilities except CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE are dropped.

So does your /etc/resolv.conf point outside of /etc or /run? 

I rather not change important security options like that.

> So does your /etc/resolv.conf point outside of /etc or /run?

Thanks, this is the root cause, my `/etc/resolve.conf` was pointing to `/var/run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf` instead of `/run/systemd/resolv/stub-resolv.conf`. I fixed it now. everything works as expected
